title: Events
description: Listen for clicks, input, and everything else.

questions.prompt: Which is the CORRECT way to listen for a click?
questions.explanation: addEventListener on the target element with the event name and handler.

questions.prompt: To stop a form's default submit action:
questions.explanation: preventDefault cancels the default browser action.

options: The element the listener is on
The element that ORIGINATED the event (where the user clicked)
questions.explanation: target = original source. currentTarget = the element the listener is on.

questions.prompt: Which stops the event from bubbling to ancestors?
questions.explanation: stopPropagation halts bubbling. preventDefault only cancels default behavior.

options: Firing events from parent to child
Listening on a parent and using e.target to identify which child was clicked
questions.explanation: One listener handles many children; scales better than binding to each.

questions.prompt: How many times is 'clicked' logged when the user clicks?
questions.explanation: { once: true } removes the listener after firing once.

questions.prompt: Which reads the value from a text input?
questions.explanation: .value is where form controls hold their current value.

questions.prompt: Which event fires as the user types?
questions.explanation: 'input' fires on every keystroke. 'change' waits for blur/commit.
